description: The ML Inference Service P99 latency has risen from 38ms to 67ms over the past two weeks as the user base grew from 300M to 400M DAU. Root cause analysis points to L2 Redis cache miss rate increasing from 8% to 22% — the feature store is being hit more frequently for user profiles that are less frequently requested (long tail of less-active users). Design a solution that brings P99 back to under 45ms without compromising the consistency model for budget data or violating GC constraints on the JVM heap.
context: Current state: L1 TTL=60s, L2 Redis TTL=5min, L2 hit rate dropped from 92% to 78%, feature store P99 latency is 180ms. JVM heap is 32GB (per ADR-006). The long-tail user problem: 30% of DAU accounts for 2% of requests — their features fall out of cache between requests. Budget enforcement cache is separate Redis cluster and must not be touched.

checks.text: Does the proposal keep the JVM heap at 32GB — not increasing it to accommodate a larger L1 cache?
Does the proposal read budget balances directly from Redis atomic counters, not from L1 or L2 cache?
Does the proposal avoid introducing per-user sticky sessions or stateful in-process stores that persist across request boundaries?

should_prune.description: Proposal that increases JVM heap from 32GB to 64GB for larger L1 cache
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-006 — heap must be 32GB; larger heaps increase ZGC evacuation pause duration and risk violating P99 tail latency constraint

should_prune.description: Proposal that reads advertiser budget balances from L1 or L2 cache to reduce Redis load
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-007 — budget checks must read from Redis atomic counters directly, never from cache

should_prune.description: Proposal that stores user ML features in a separate stateful in-process store that persists across requests
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-001 — request-handling services must not hold per-user state beyond a single request lifetime

should_prune.description: Proposal that uses platform threads instead of virtual threads for additional concurrency
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-006 — request handling must use Java 21 virtual threads, not platform threads

should_pass: Proposal that addresses the long-tail cache miss problem by pre-warming ML feature cache during off-peak hours or increasing ML feature L2 TTL (not budget cache TTL), without increasing JVM heap and without introducing sticky sessions
